title: Trace Relationships through a Data Table
summary: Trace Relationships through a Data Table retrieves prior skills in an original regional heat-service capacity reporting desk scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply table structure, table header associations in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real regional heat-service capacity reporting desk artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

steps.title: 04 · Read the regional heat-service capacity reporting desk contract · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 4: Which practice survives changed data and devices? Scenario: regional heat-service capacity reporting desk.
steps.why: Trace Relationships through a Data Table makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 4 in regional heat-service capacity reporting desk.

content.text: A data table represents relationships across rows and columns. Caption names the table’s purpose. Thead, tbody, and tfoot group meaningful row regions. Tr contains a row; th identifies a header; td contains data. Tables are not page-layout grids, and visual borders do not create data relationships. Header cells create programmatic context. Scope col and scope row are clear for simple tables. Complex matrices may require stable header ids and headers references so each data cell identifies all relevant headers. Bold text or a first-row visual style cannot replace th relationships. Apply it to regional heat-service capacity reporting desk.
